DECLARATION OF MICHAEL T. KENNICK, ESQ. AND EXHIBIT IN SUPPORT OF TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC'S RESPONSE TO PLTF'S STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS FILED BY DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER DEFENDANT RATIFIED THE TORTIOUS CONDUCT OF SAMUEL. PLAINTIFF REPORTED SAMUEL'S THREATS OF VIOLENCE TO HER EMPLOYER (PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION 89:13-90:9; 70:5-71:13), SAMUEL WAS KNOWN TO BE TAKING DRUGS THAT AFFECTED HER BEHAVIOR (LOPEZ DEPOSITION 26:3-11 AND MCELROY DEPOSITION 43:9-16), AND THE EMPLOYER FAILED TO INTERVENE WITH THE EXCEPTION OF ONE TALK BY CARRIGAN. (PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITON 89:13-90:9 AND CARRIGAN DEPOSITION 107:1-110:9). DEFENDANT'S EVID. OBJ., 1 & 2, ARE OVERRULED, & 3, 4 & 5 ARE SUSTAINED; PLFT'S OBJECTIONS TO CARRIGAN DECL. ARE OVERRULED. (ORDER RE: RULINGS RE: EVIDENTIARY OBJECTS. TO DECLARATION OF DIJADA DURDEN ARE FILED IN OPEN COURT.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: JOANN BRYCE, CSR #3321

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-24-2011 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing (As To Defaulted Defendant Chanel Samuel).
Off Calendar Aug-31-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Michael Begert, Clerk: J. Willis-McGhee,
CSR: A. Howell #3542

William Weiss, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Mydra McGarr.
Plainiff was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Chanel Samuel.

Default was entered against defendant on December 7, 2010.
Tenderloin Housing Clinic and all Doe defendants were dismissed on February 11, 2010.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Mydra McGarr, and against the Defaulted Defendant Chanel Samuel as follows:

Special Damages $750,000.00
Damages - $3,000,000.00
Special Damages 1,000,000.00
Special Medical Damages $250,000.00
Future Medical Damages $100,000
Wage Loss $50,000.00
Future Wage Loss $150,000.00
for a total judgment in the amount of $4,600,450.00.

Plaintiff to prepare judgment.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: William E. Weiss, Law Offices of William E. Weiss, (415) 362-6765, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Michael T. Kennick, Esq. of Kennick & Associates (714) 992-6600, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment is denied. Triable issue of fact raised whether defendant ratified the tortious conduct of Samuel. Plaintiff reported Samuel's threats of violence to her employer (Plaintiff's deposition 89:13-90:9; 70:5-71:13), Samuel was known to be taking drugs that affected her behavior (Lopez deposition 26:3-11 and McElroy deposition 43:9-16), and the employer failed to intervene with the exception of one talk by Carrigan. (Plaintiff's depositon 89:13-90:9 and Carrigan deposition 107:1-110:9). Defendant's Evid. Obj., 1 & 2, are overruled, & 3, 4 & 5 are Sustained; Plft's Objections to Carrigan Decl. are overruled. (Order re: Rulings re: Evidentiary Objects. to Declaration of Dijada Durden are filed in Open Court.)(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: JoAnn Bryce CSR 3321
